questions.text: Why does the Surah begin with dramatic changes in the universe?
questions.answer: To shake human complacency and show that Allah can overturn the world’s order to begin judgment.

questions.text: True/False: Surah At-Takwir teaches that the universe is stable by itself and cannot change.
questions.answer: False. It shows Allah can change the sun, stars, mountains, and sky at His command.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about ‘what a soul has brought’?
questions.answer: Every person will realize their deeds and intentions and face their true outcome.

questions.text: What moral lesson is taught by the question asked about the buried infant girl?
questions.answer: Allah’s justice exposes ظلم and honors victims; unjust cultural practices will be judged.

questions.text: How does this Surah correct harmful cultural practices using moral accountability?
questions.answer: It shows even socially accepted ظلم becomes a crime before Allah and will be questioned.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests that crimes against the weak can be hidden forever.
questions.answer: False. The records are opened and hidden ظلم is exposed.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the records of deeds being opened?
questions.answer: Accountability is public and clear; people cannot deny what they did.

questions.text: What is one spiritual lesson from the image of people neglecting their most valuable possessions?
questions.answer: Dunya loses its value when the Hereafter arrives; real value is in deeds.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about Angel Jibreel’s role in revelation?
questions.answer: He is a noble messenger who conveys revelation reliably by Allah’s command.

questions.text: Why does the Surah describe Jibreel with strength and high rank?
questions.answer: To show the revelation is protected, honorable, and not a human invention.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Prophet’s mental soundness and honesty?
questions.answer: It rejects accusations of madness and affirms he faithfully conveys what he receives.

questions.text: True/False: Surah At-Takwir teaches that the Quran comes from devils.
questions.answer: False. It explicitly rejects that and affirms it is revelation.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Quran being a reminder for all worlds?
questions.answer: Its guidance is universal and not restricted to one tribe or era.

questions.text: How does this Surah balance human choice with Allah’s will?
questions.answer: People choose the straight path, but their ability and guidance are ultimately under Allah’s will.

questions.text: What is one practical meaning of ‘take the straight path’ for daily life?
questions.answer: Choose obedience, honesty, and repentance consistently instead of drifting with desires.

questions.text: What is one lesson from Allah swearing by stars and the night/day transitions?
questions.answer: Allah points to precise signs in creation to strengthen certainty and reflection.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that reflection on creation is unrelated to faith.
questions.answer: False. It uses creation as proof and reminder.

questions.text: How can Surah At-Takwir help someone who feels spiritually numb?
questions.answer: Its vivid scenes of the Last Day break complacency and renew seriousness and repentance.

questions.text: How does the Surah teach dignity and justice for women and the vulnerable?
questions.answer: It condemns ظلم against vulnerable girls and shows Allah questions such crimes.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about rumors and accusations against prophets?
questions.answer: False accusations are rejected; prophets convey truth with integrity.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that powerful people can rewrite truth through propaganda.
questions.answer: False. Allah exposes falsehood and preserves revelation.

questions.text: What does 'At-Takwir' mean in the context of the sun?
questions.answer: It means the sun being wrapped up, folded, or lose its light.

questions.text: What will happen to the stars on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will fall away and lose their luster.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the mountains on that Day?
questions.answer: They will be moved and set in motion (as if made to walk).

questions.text: What is the significance of 'the ten-month pregnant camels' being neglected?
questions.answer: It symbolizes people abandoning their most precious worldly possessions out of terror.

questions.text: What will happen to the wild beasts?
questions.answer: They will be gathered together.

questions.text: What is the 'Maw'udah' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The female infant buried alive in pre-Islamic times.

questions.text: What question will be asked regarding the buried infant?
questions.answer: For what crime/sin she was killed.

questions.text: What will happen to the records of deeds?
questions.answer: They will be laid open/unrolled for everyone to see.

questions.text: What does this Surah mention happens to the sky?
questions.answer: It is stripped away or pulled off (Kushitat).

questions.text: When will a soul know what it has brought of deeds?
questions.answer: When the celestial events occur and the Hellfire is set ablaze.

questions.text: Allah swears by 'the retreating stars' (Al-Khunnas). What are they?
questions.answer: Stars or planets that recede, run their course, and hide/set.

questions.text: What does 'the night as it dissipates' (As'as) refer to?
questions.answer: The transition of the night as it begins or ends.

questions.text: Whose word is the Quran described as being in this Surah?
questions.answer: The word of a noble Messenger (Angel Jibreel).

questions.text: What are three descriptions of Angel Jibreel given in this Surah?
questions.answer: Possessor of power, secure in position with the Lord of the Throne, and obeyed by other angels.

questions.text: Does the Prophet (SAW) have a companion who is mad?
questions.answer: No, Allah refutes this by saying 'Your companion is not mad'.

questions.text: Where did the Prophet (SAW) see Angel Jibreel?
questions.answer: On the clear horizon.

questions.text: Is the Prophet (SAW) withholding of the knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb)?
questions.answer: No, he is not a withholder (Danin) of the revelation.

questions.text: Whose word is the Quran NOT, according to this Surah?
questions.answer: It is not the word of an expelled/accursed devil.

questions.text: To whom is the Quran a reminder?
questions.answer: For all the worlds (al-Alamin).

questions.text: Can a person choose to take the straight path by their own will alone?
questions.answer: Only if Allah, the Lord of the worlds, wills it.
